reconciliation.sole_pool: etcgem sMOMENT/sector pool (P_total x f_metab x sigma, medium/growth-law/kcat aware)
reconciliation.note: the two pools were two computations of the same ~0.1 g/gDW enzyme-mass quantity; kept the sector one

honest_residual: The fixable redundancy unlocks the magnitude levers (kcat/sigma now MOVE the ceiling instead of saturating against the GECKO pool at 1.55). But reaching the observed ~2.4 still needs ~1.7x more total enzyme budget (sigma ~0.45 -> ~0.77), ABOVE the 0.4-0.5 literature range -- so part of the rich-medium magnitude is a genuinely higher in-vivo capacity (and the observed 2.4 is a figure-digitized high-end value), not just the pool double-accounting.
